summary: Typosquat of @langchain/langgraph exfiltrates developer identity and cloud config on install
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and harvests installer-side identity and cloud-configuration data, then POSTs a JSON payload to a hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint at https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. Collected fields include OS hostname and username, git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and the project's.git/config, recent committer emails from.git/logs/HEAD, gh CLI identity from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, SSH public-key comment emails from ~/.ssh/*.pub, the search domain from /etc/resolv.conf, GCP project and account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, and the consuming project's package.json metadata. The package name '@langgraphjs/toolkit' impersonates the legitimate @langchain/langgraph (LangGraph.js) namespace and declares @langchain/langgraph as a dependency to appear functional. The 'telemetry' framing in the script is a cover story — the payload contains direct personal and cloud-account identifiers harvested from installer-owned paths the package never wrote.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
